mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series grow concurrently, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of monetary tightening reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 20-day returns in >55% of tightening periods, or the predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity that boosts near-term corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 315.0, it indicates entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 315.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real personal consumption expenditures grow faster than real GDP signals consumption-led economic expansion that disproportionately benefits large-cap equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The consumption-GDP growth differential fails to predict positive returns, or the interaction effect reverses sign during periods of fiscal contraction.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals premature supply chain contraction that triggers immediate profit-taking, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in <40% of instances when INDPRO declines during recovery phases, or the signal shows no predictive edge in walk-forward testing.
